title: Gougères
subtitle: Savoury cheese choux puffs
excerpt: The Burgundian aperitif puff: choux pastry beaten with grated Gruyère and Parmesan, piped in walnut-sized rounds, and baked until deep gold. Twenty-four gougères, served warm with wine.
sourceNotes: Marie-Antoine Carême (1833) and Auguste Escoffier (1903): document the gougère as a Burgundian regional savoury choux. The household form (cheese beaten into the choux paste before piping) is documented across pre-1928 French sources.

recipe.freezeNotes: Bake, cool, freeze in a sealed bag; refresh in a 160°C oven for 5 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake fresh and serve warm; they hold well for 30 minutes after baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — drops to 170°C for the last 10 minutes

content.text: Gougères are the savoury cousin of the cream puff: the same choux pastry method (boil, cook out flour, beat in eggs) but with grated Gruyère and Parmesan beaten through the paste at the end. Piped in walnut-sized rounds and baked, they puff up hollow and crisp, with the cheese giving a deep gold colour and a savoury edge.
A Burgundian aperitif. Serve warm with a glass of chilled white wine. Cold gougères revive in a hot oven for two or three minutes.

items.prepNote: large, beaten; use to the egg-test cue

content.text: Boil, cook out, beat in eggs

Heat the oven to 200°C fan and line two baking trays. Bring the {{water}}, {{whole-milk}}, {{unsalted-butter}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} to a rolling boil. Take off the heat and tip in the sifted {{plain-flour}}; beat hard to a smooth paste. Return to a low heat and beat for 2 minutes until the paste leaves the pan clean. Tip into a bowl. Beat in the {{eggs}} a quarter at a time until the paste flows in a slow ribbon and holds a brief V on the spoon.

Beat in three-quarters of the {{gruyere}}, all the {{parmesan}}, the {{black-pepper-ground}} and the {{nutmeg-ground}}. Fill a piping bag with a 12 mm round tip. Pipe 24 walnut-sized rounds onto the trays with 4 cm between each. Scatter the reserved Gruyère over the tops.
Bake at 200°C fan for 18 minutes, then drop to 170°C for another 10 minutes; the puffs should be deep gold, the cheese should be melted, and they should feel firm and light when lifted. Serve warm.
